foundational_legal_framework.note: The exploitation regulations must be fully consistent with UNCLOS (LOSC 1982) and the 1994 IA. The IA prevails over Part XI of UNCLOS in case of inconsistency (Article 2, 1994 IA). Together they form a single instrument.

section_1_costs_institutional.subject: Costs to States Parties and Institutional Arrangements
section_1_costs_institutional.change: Streamlined the Authority's initial structure and reduced costs. Limited the Enterprise's initial operations.

section_2_enterprise.subject: The Enterprise
section_2_enterprise.change: Enterprise no longer required to begin operations concurrently with ISA. Must initially operate through joint ventures with private contractors.

section_3_decision_making.subject: Decision-making in the Council
section_3_decision_making.change: Revised Council voting procedures. Consensus must be sought before any vote. Revised Chamber composition for voting majorities.

section_4_review_conference.subject: Review Conference
section_4_review_conference.change: Eliminated the mandatory 15-year Review Conference. A review conference may be convened but is no longer automatic.

section_5_transfer_of_technology.subject: Transfer of Technology
section_5_transfer_of_technology.change: Mandatory technology transfer obligation significantly weakened. Now only on 'fair and reasonable commercial terms and conditions' on a market basis.

section_6_production_policies.subject: Production Policies
section_6_production_policies.change: Anti-protectionist production limitations replaced with general obligation to promote vigorous competition and prevent monopolisation.

section_7_economic_assistance.subject: Economic Assistance to Developing States
section_7_economic_assistance.change: Mandatory economic assistance fund replaced with a more flexible mechanism under the Finance Committee.

section_8_finance_committee.subject: Finance Committee (new body)
section_8_finance_committee.change: Created a new Finance Committee with decision-making by consensus. States with the largest assessed contributions have disproportionate influence.

section_9_financial_terms.subject: Financial Terms of Contracts
section_9_financial_terms.change: Simplified the complex two-tier financial system (fixed fee + profit-sharing). Contractors may now choose between a royalty system or a profit-sharing system.

documents.title: United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea
documents.description: The foundational 'constitution for the oceans'. Part XI (Articles 133–191) establishes the international seabed area as the Common Heritage of Mankind, creates the International Seabed Authority (ISA), and sets the legal framework for all exploitation activities in the Area. NOTE: Several provisions of Part XI have been modified or replaced by the 1994 Implementation Agreement, which prevails in case of inconsistency.

part_xi_key_articles.137: Legal status of the Area and its resources
part_xi_key_articles.141: Use of the Area exclusively for peaceful purposes
part_xi_key_articles.142: Rights and legitimate interests of coastal States
part_xi_key_articles.150: Policies relating to activities in the Area [MODIFIED BY IA]
part_xi_key_articles.161: Composition, procedure and voting in the Council [MODIFIED BY IA]

documents.title: Agreement relating to the Implementation of Part XI of the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea of 10 December 1982
documents.description: The 1994 Agreement fundamentally reformed Part XI of UNCLOS to make it acceptable to industrialised states. Under Article 2, the IA and Part XI are interpreted and applied as a single instrument. In case of inconsistency, the IA PREVAILS. Ratification of UNCLOS after 1994 automatically constitutes consent to the IA. The Annex to the IA contains 9 sections modifying specific UNCLOS Part XI provisions — see foundational_legal_framework.part_xi_articles_modified_by_ia for the full breakdown.
documents.legal_relationship_to_unclos: PREVAILS over UNCLOS Part XI in case of inconsistency (Article 2(1)). Both instruments are interpreted and applied together as a single instrument (Article 2(1)). Consent to UNCLOS after 28 July 1994 also constitutes consent to the IA.

documents.title: Consolidation of Part XI of the Convention and the Implementation Agreement
documents.description: ISA-published e-book (2025) presenting UNCLOS Part XI and the 1994 Implementation Agreement as a consolidated single instrument, showing which UNCLOS provisions are modified by the IA. This is the most practical reference for the exploitation regulations drafting process, as it shows the applicable law in consolidated form.
